full_prompt: Design a monolithic forged and machined 7075-T6 aluminum front traction-motor mount bracket for a collegiate EV powertrain integration benchmark (premium crossover EV platform), attaching a 180 kW permanent-magnet synchronous motor (mass 65 kg) to the vehicle front subframe. The bracket is a single forged body (no welds, no inserts, no bolted gussets) with 4 through-holes on the motor-side face and 3 through-holes on the subframe-side face. Motor-side pattern: four M12 clearance holes (13 mm diameter) on a 180 mm x 180 mm rectangular pattern, on a 25 mm thick flat flange 220 mm x 220 mm mating to the motor housing. Subframe-side pattern: three M14 clearance holes (15 mm diameter) on a triangle pattern with 110 mm base and 95 mm height, on a 20 mm thick flat flange 150 mm x 130 mm. Vertical distance (motor axis to subframe mating surface) 170 mm. Overall bounding envelope 260 mm x 240 mm x 200 mm. Material 7075-T6 with E 71.7 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2810 kg/m^3, yield 503 MPa, UTS 572 MPa. Three load cases derived from OEM body-structure loads spec for the vehicle platform (representative SAE J670 axis: x = longitudinal, y = lateral, z = vertical). Load Case 1 (peak torque reaction): 420 N-m peak motor torque reacted at the motor-side flange as a couple about the motor axis (x), combined with motor self-weight 65 kg * 2g vertical bump = 1276 N downward. Load Case 2 (crash pulse): 35g longitudinal (x-direction) deceleration per FMVSS 208 / OEM internal EV crash calibration, motor self-weight 65 kg produces 22.3 kN longitudinal force applied at the motor-side face centroid. Load Case 3 (NVH durability): modal input at the motor-side face with 10 g sinusoidal sweep 20-2000 Hz, to assess mount stiffness k_x, k_y, k_z as equivalent static deflections at 1 kN per axis. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 (operating) peak von Mises must not exceed 251 MPa (yield/2.0, standard OEM body-structure operating SF); (R2) under LC2 crash, peak stress must not exceed 335 MPa (yield/1.5 ultimate SF for crash, short transient); (R3) for NVH under LC3, the mount equivalent stiffness in each axis must be between 15-50 kN/mm (neither too soft to cause powertrain droop resonance nor too stiff to transmit whine), AND first six natural frequencies of the bracket + motor-mass system must have a margin of at least 15% around the 24x electric-motor rotor harmonic at rated speed 12000 rpm = 200 Hz and 200 x 24 = 4800 Hz (check the band 170-230 Hz and 4080-5520 Hz); (R4) a buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC2 compressive crash must yield a first-mode factor of at least 1.8 per OEM crash bracket criterion; (R5) mass of the bracket must not exceed 2.8 kg per BOM mass budget allocated by the benchmark cost-and-mass table; (R6) maximum displacement at the motor axis centerline under LC1 must not exceed 0.3 mm to avoid driveline half-shaft angular misalignment. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic forged-and-machined 7075-T6 aluminum front traction-motor mount bracket for a collegiate EV powertrain integration benchmark (premium crossover), attaching a 180 kW PMSM (65 kg) to the vehicle front subframe. The bracket is a single forged body with two integral bolt-pattern flanges (motor-side 4-hole, subframe-side 3-hole triangle). Apply the usual yield/2.0 operating and yield/1.5 crash-transient safety factors (SAE J670 axes: x long, y lat, z vert).

Envelope: roughly 260 x 240 x 200 mm with a ~170 mm vertical distance from the motor axis to the subframe mating face.

Load cases.
- LC1 peak torque + bump: 420 N-m peak motor torque reacted as a couple about the motor axis, superposed with 1.28 kN vertical (motor self-weight at 2 g bump).
- LC2 crash pulse: 35 g longitudinal on the 65 kg motor mass = 22.3 kN longitudinal force at the motor-side flange centroid.
- LC3 NVH / stiffness: 1 kN per axis at the motor-side face to extract equivalent mount stiffness, plus a modal check against the electric-motor 24x rotor harmonic at 12000 rpm (170-230 Hz and 4080-5520 Hz bands).

prompt.title: 7075-T6 traction motor mount for collegiate EV powertrain integration benchmark
prompt.brief: Forged bracket linking 180 kW PMSM (65 kg) to vehicle front subframe; survive 35g crash + 420 N-m torque reaction; NVH-tuned stiffness.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Mount is cooled by ambient; motor heat handled by coolant.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Bolt preload relaxation with coating creep

eval_coverage: OEM body-structure operating vs crash SF
PMSM electric motor harmonic resonance avoidance
